OUTFIT_1_FLAVORTEXT: Worn by apprentice wizards, the Magi's Shroud is a light, yet magical garb.
OUTFIT_2_FLAVORTEXT: A robe made from magical silk, used by student and teacher alike at the Academy.
OUTFIT_3_FLAVORTEXT: The bells on this cloak only ring when its wearer wins a battle, never when they walk.
OUTFIT_4_FLAVORTEXT: It is common to see this fine colorful coat worn by fancy folk at formal gatherings. Or by pirates.
OUTFIT_5_FLAVORTEXT: Only the most powerful wizards wear this enchanted robe, adorned with eagle's feathers.
OUTFIT_6_FLAVORTEXT: It is said the ice crystals used to make this robe will protect its wearer from bad things.
OUTFIT_7_FLAVORTEXT: Made from the hide of "Carcus the Terrible" the most unforgiving dragon ever known.
OUTFIT_8_FLAVORTEXT: This cloak can sense its wearer and will burn more brightly when it detects fear.
OUTFIT_9_FLAVORTEXT: The standard robe worn by initiated members of the Academy's Water School.
OUTFIT_10_FLAVORTEXT: The bright robe worn only by those who have braved Bonfire Spires many dangers.
OUTFIT_11_FLAVORTEXT: The standard robe worn by initiated members of the Academy's Storm School.
OUTFIT_12_FLAVORTEXT: The standard robe worn by initiated members of the Academy's Earth School.
OUTFIT_13_FLAVORTEXT: Worn for practice dueling, it is not uncommon to see this garb singed and torn.
OUTFIT_14_FLAVORTEXT: The favourite choice of wizards on hot days, it's cool but provides little protection.
OUTFIT_15_FLAVORTEXT: Light and durable, this robe is prized by wizards both young and old for its comfort.
OUTFIT_16_FLAVORTEXT: Made for wizards who don't like being cold but don't mind being itchy.
OUTFIT_17_FLAVORTEXT: For the stylish wizard, these robes are intimidating and stain-resistant!
OUTFIT_18_FLAVORTEXT: Modern wizards love how soft these robes feel, often worn by academics in libraries.
OUTFIT_19_FLAVORTEXT: The Magi were ancient wizards that studied the stars. They wore these robes for their most important ceremonies.
OUTFIT_20_FLAVORTEXT: This is the product of genius weavers who were able to spin metal into colorful threads.
OUTFIT_21_FLAVORTEXT: As strong as it is lightweight, Mithril was once rare because it could only be found in distant lands.
OUTFIT_22_FLAVORTEXT: This robe gets its name from its large blue gems, often referred to as "Water Glass".
OUTFIT_23_FLAVORTEXT: Specially designed for a northern wizard who studied the history of magic, Culix's diaries are required reading for student wizards.
OUTFIT_24_FLAVORTEXT: Only the Trial Masters of past and present were given these robes and are forbidden to take them off. How did you get them?
OUTFIT_25_FLAVORTEXT: Need to keep warm in those winter days? It may not be a robe, but it sure is warm!
OUTFIT_26_FLAVORTEXT: The official outfit worn by helpers at Winter Fest!
OUTFIT_27_FLAVORTEXT: In close combat these spikes are dangerous indeed. Be careful when you're around your friends!
OUTFIT_28_FLAVORTEXT: Commonly worn in very cold places, these robes are made from many layers of fur and pelts.
OUTFIT_29_FLAVORTEXT: A magical robe made from the leaves and materials found in Firefly Forest.
OUTFIT_30_FLAVORTEXT: These robes were custom made for a wizard...but they seem to fit you just right!
OUTFIT_31_FLAVORTEXT: These wooly robes will keep you warm in cold weather...but don't wear them in the sun!
OUTFIT_32_FLAVORTEXT: The robes of one of the greatest wizards in the Academy.
OUTFIT_33_FLAVORTEXT: The ceremonial robes of the Shiverchill Tribes.
OUTFIT_34_FLAVORTEXT: These can only be worn by those who have seen the "Bonfire of the Spire", a volcanic eruption, up close.
OUTFIT_35_FLAVORTEXT: Don't these just scream "Arggg"? They even come with the optional hook attachment!
OUTFIT_36_FLAVORTEXT: The choice clothes of deckhands and mates everywhere. Watch out, captains will be sure to shout orders at you.
OUTFIT_37_FLAVORTEXT: Humans that come up to the clouds wear this outfit to stay warm and dry.
OUTFIT_38_FLAVORTEXT: These robes give off immense power, and almost seem to repel the ground below you.
OUTFIT_39_FLAVORTEXT: This is the outfit of the Oasis Institute of Natural Creatures (OINC).
OUTFIT_40_FLAVORTEXT: These robes protect the wearer from the brutal weather in the desert.
OUTFIT_41_FLAVORTEXT: This outfit is very stylish, but also pretty heavy-duty! The people back then sure were tough!
OUTFIT_42_FLAVORTEXT: The jumpsuit worn by the official TEK-Y4 dance instructors. Kind of sweaty...
OUTFIT_43_FLAVORTEXT: Whoever said that a wizard couldn't be a robot never had a chance to try these on...
OUTFIT_44_FLAVORTEXT: The Pumpkin Lord is said to return every year on Pumpkinfest to rule over all pumpkins.
OUTFIT_45_FLAVORTEXT: A cute Luminite costume! It's so lifelike!
OUTFIT_46_FLAVORTEXT: The tattered robes of the legendary specter - a ghost that only shows up around Pumpkinfest.
OUTFIT_47_FLAVORTEXT: This warm, rugged coat is equipped with a standard set of climbing equipment, a must have for exploring the mountains.
OUTFIT_48_FLAVORTEXT: The magic armor of the great Viking Frost Beard - a mystic wind constantly billows its cape.
OUTFIT_49_FLAVORTEXT: Who knew that Yetis shed?! Whether the coat directly came from a Yeti or was crafted from loose hairs is unclear.
OUTFIT_50_FLAVORTEXT: This armor is lighter than it looks, but it seems to be all for show...the metal actually seems to be plastic...
OUTFIT_51_FLAVORTEXT: A magic barrier seems to emanate from the cloak...protecting you from all sorts of nasty spells.
OUTFIT_52_FLAVORTEXT: Worn by ancient battle wizards, this gear seems to fit you perfectly, almost as if you were meant to wear it.
OUTFIT_53_FLAVORTEXT: This outfit is worn only by the greatest of Bounty Hunters. It is a testament to the skill of its wearer.
OUTFIT_54_FLAVORTEXT: The standard-issue garb of a Bounty Hunter. It holds up in the toughest of situations.
OUTFIT_55_FLAVORTEXT: Fly like a bee and sting like a bee'. Wait, that's not right...
OUTFIT_56_FLAVORTEXT: This armor was used by Candle Knights for protection.
OUTFIT_57_FLAVORTEXT: Just the thing to wear if you are planning on fighting rodents that are quite large.
OUTFIT_58_FLAVORTEXT: They're made from a material that mimics the magic-resistance of jellyfish. Commonly worn during festivals-at-sea.
OUTFIT_59_FLAVORTEXT: Keeps the water out and your gases in. You have been warned.
OUTFIT_60_FLAVORTEXT: The plates of the suit are flexible, so it offers protection without making it hard to move.
OUTFIT_61_FLAVORTEXT: This old coat belonged to the admiral who led the charge against the evil Order of Influence many years ago.

ULTIMATESQUESTITEM_52_FLAVORTEXT: These starfish are friends of Hydroid that got lost. They'll help you find Hydroid.
ULTIMATESQUESTITEM_53_FLAVORTEXT: Hydroid likes to relax at the end of the day with an iced seaweed tea.
ULTIMATESQUESTITEM_54_FLAVORTEXT: A safety device used by Hydroid during their ocean rescue adventures.
ULTIMATESQUESTITEM_55_FLAVORTEXT: The tarot cards give Luna clues about past, present, and future.
ULTIMATESQUESTITEM_56_FLAVORTEXT: These are Luna's favorite flowers. They sleep during the day and grow in the moonlight.
ULTIMATESQUESTITEM_57_FLAVORTEXT: Luna uses these special round crystals from Shiverchill Mountain to look into the future.
ULTIMATESQUESTITEM_64_FLAVORTEXT: Ancient coral that can be found in rivers and lakes.
ULTIMATESQUESTITEM_65_FLAVORTEXT: A small sea creature that cleans Gryphtopus's tentacles.
ULTIMATESQUESTITEM_67_FLAVORTEXT: These large and strong feathers are what allow Merahawk to take to the skies.
ULTIMATESQUESTITEM_68_FLAVORTEXT: A large nest with a few colorful Merahawk eggs.
ULTIMATESQUESTITEM_69_FLAVORTEXT: A toy Clovrat that Merahawk likes to play with.
ULTIMATESQUESTITEM_70_FLAVORTEXT: Shiny pearls strung along fine rope.
ULTIMATESQUESTITEM_71_FLAVORTEXT: A sand sculpture made to look like Shimmerstorm.
ULTIMATESQUESTITEM_72_FLAVORTEXT: A pile of beautiful rocks laying in soft sand.
ULTIMATESQUESTITEM_76_FLAVORTEXT: Some clawmarks can be found scratch into this snowboard.
ULTIMATESQUESTITEM_77_FLAVORTEXT: Snow falls in this snowglobe even when sitting still.
ULTIMATESQUESTITEM_78_FLAVORTEXT: This tuft of fur somehow feels frozen yet soft.
ULTIMATESQUESTITEM_79_FLAVORTEXT: A snowglobe with a cute snowmoth inside.
ULTIMATESQUESTITEM_80_FLAVORTEXT: A frozen piece of delicate silk.
ULTIMATESQUESTITEM_81_FLAVORTEXT: A cracked cocoon frozen in time.
WEAPON_1_FLAVORTEXT: The most unassuming of wands, it is said to be imbued with natural energy.
WEAPON_2_FLAVORTEXT: Just like the stars themselves, it is most powerful under the cover of night.
WEAPON_3_FLAVORTEXT: Charmed with a celestial power, wizards take it to be a symbol of justice and good.
WEAPON_4_FLAVORTEXT: Its orb pulsates when close to the ocean and water pets hum gently in its presence.
WEAPON_5_FLAVORTEXT: Often given to young wizards who love that it squawks when it casts a spell.
WEAPON_6_FLAVORTEXT: Hold on tight! Many unsuspecting wizards have watched this wand fly away.
WEAPON_7_FLAVORTEXT: Made for a wizard who was also a werewolf, it strengthens as the moon approaches full.
WEAPON_8_FLAVORTEXT: Friend to the wizard who travels far from home, it comforts a lonely heart and smells of fresh bread.
WEAPON_9_FLAVORTEXT: It was once a wizard's pet Phoenix that relinquished its magic to empower his wand. It saved him from peril most great.
WEAPON_10_FLAVORTEXT: The wand used to end the reign of "Carcus the Terrible", its origins are a mystery.
WEAPON_11_FLAVORTEXT: To save her village, a wizard once ran into the eye of a storm. When it abated, all that remained was this wand.
WEAPON_12_FLAVORTEXT: The wand that belonged to Crios as a child, he cast it aside as "weak" when he entered the Academy.
WEAPON_13_FLAVORTEXT: Cold to the touch, wizards who use it shiver uncontrollably and their lips turn blue.
WEAPON_14_FLAVORTEXT: Whoever possesses this wand will never have a dreamless sleep and when they dream of the future, it always comes true.
WEAPON_15_FLAVORTEXT: During the winter its leaf withers and falls off but always grows again the following spring.
WEAPON_16_FLAVORTEXT: This staff was once paired with another just like it and belonged to twin wizards.
WEAPON_17_FLAVORTEXT: Energizing whoever holds it, this staff was forged entirely from the earth by ancient wizards using a method now lost.
WEAPON_18_FLAVORTEXT: Commonly used by the Flameweavers of the Spire to create beautiful burning patterns in the night sky.
WEAPON_19_FLAVORTEXT: Long sought after by pirates who can use it to push their ships through the waves.
WEAPON_20_FLAVORTEXT: Its central gem comes from deep in the Shiverchill Mountains, allowing wizards to turn the water in air to ice.
WEAPON_21_FLAVORTEXT: Used by the Dragon Tamers of old, no one knows if it retains its power to control the winged beasts. Do you dare find out?
WEAPON_22_FLAVORTEXT: Containing the power of the Phoenix, some say the one who holds it can recover from any injury.
WEAPON_23_FLAVORTEXT: Made by a Dreamweaver, if used correctly, it can put your opponent into a deep sleep.
WEAPON_24_FLAVORTEXT: Its wielder need have no fear of the dark. The power of the forest makes everything visible.
WEAPON_25_FLAVORTEXT: Also known as the "Safety Wand", it is used by all academy wizards to reduce injury during training.
WEAPON_26_FLAVORTEXT: A creative wizard carved this himself from a branch in a nearby forest.
WEAPON_27_FLAVORTEXT: A geologist wizard once claimed that a wand could be made from rocks, this wand proves it!
WEAPON_28_FLAVORTEXT: Though rough to the touch and very heavy, its got plenty of power for the everyday wizard.
WEAPON_29_FLAVORTEXT: Forged from various metals by blacksmiths, some wizards feel that its the best material for wands.
WEAPON_30_FLAVORTEXT: Its dark stone sparkles with shadow magic, like stars in the night sky.
WEAPON_31_FLAVORTEXT: The diamond in this wand has not been shaped or cut, leaving its earthly energy untouched.
WEAPON_32_FLAVORTEXT: Though it looks like gold, it's not. Pyrium is prized for its strength and flexibility, making this a very durable wand.
WEAPON_33_FLAVORTEXT: Not as rare as it once was, Mithril is still popular amongst the wizards of distant lands.
WEAPON_34_FLAVORTEXT: Students of the stars, the Magi loved the setting sun most of all, making this their favorite wand.
WEAPON_35_FLAVORTEXT: Its strength is unsurpassed by any other metal wand. It has been known to block even the most potent of spells.
WEAPON_36_FLAVORTEXT: Some say that staffs are more powerful but are also harder to use, making this staff very important for training.
WEAPON_37_FLAVORTEXT: In the old days wizards would carve their own staffs from the wood of the forest, this one seems quite old.
WEAPON_38_FLAVORTEXT: Only the strongest wizards can carry the weight of this staff, giving it the nickname the "Staff of Giants".
WEAPON_39_FLAVORTEXT: Used by the monks of the ancient lands, many have adopted its use because of its common materials and uncommon strength.
WEAPON_40_FLAVORTEXT: The choice of wizard knights of old, who could carry the lightweight staff with all their heavy armor on.
WEAPON_41_FLAVORTEXT: It's smooth and polished to shine. It's clear a fine craftsman made this beautiful staff.
WEAPON_42_FLAVORTEXT: Diamond is very hard and very sharp, it's recommended that you wear gloves when using this staff.
WEAPON_43_FLAVORTEXT: Pyrium is difficult to work with. It must have taken a great blacksmith to make this staff.
WEAPON_44_FLAVORTEXT: This staff has an aura of old power around it. Did it come from somewhere far away?
WEAPON_45_FLAVORTEXT: The Magi liked staffs over wands because they were longer and they reached closer to the stars.
WEAPON_46_FLAVORTEXT: Made from the strongest of metals, its beauty is only matched by its power.
WEAPON_47_FLAVORTEXT: To be held only by the Trial Masters, its powers are the stuff of legends. How did you get it?
WEAPON_48_FLAVORTEXT: Its sound is beautiful and is used by helpers of Winter Fest to lull the crowds with its enchanting tones.
WEAPON_49_FLAVORTEXT: A prize given to those who have successfully navigated the Labyrinth, it prevents them from ever being lost again.
WEAPON_50_FLAVORTEXT: A ball of energy from the sun itself.
WEAPON_51_FLAVORTEXT: Designed to appear like the mythical Flamerose, a rare flower that grows only on the side of the Bonfire Spire.
WEAPON_52_FLAVORTEXT: Transparent, it's easy to admire the beauty of the energy being charged inside this wand just before a spell is cast.
WEAPON_53_FLAVORTEXT: Those who hold this wand can sense what is around them even in the darkest of caves - much like the bat, which gives the wand its name.
WEAPON_54_FLAVORTEXT: Many wizards refuse to make their wands from living trees, instead they grow this wand from the forest's soils.
WEAPON_55_FLAVORTEXT: For those who hail from the Spire, this wand that carries a flame from Bonfire is respected and admired.
WEAPON_56_FLAVORTEXT: Its heat is so immense that its owner must possess great knowledge of fire magic simply to be in its presence.
WEAPON_57_FLAVORTEXT: In the hands of the ice king of old, this staff was used to turn those who insulted his beard into frozen statues.
WEAPON_58_FLAVORTEXT: As beautiful as it is elegant, it can be used to break light into parts that can be weaved into powerful spells.
WEAPON_59_FLAVORTEXT: An ancient staff from the realms of the Shiverchill tribes, it is thought to possess an animal spirit.
WEAPON_60_FLAVORTEXT: Also known as the "Heart of the Dragon", the limits of this staff's power have never been reached.
WEAPON_61_FLAVORTEXT: This staff was grown by tree whisperers from the roots of the elder trees deep in the heart of the Firefly Forest.
WEAPON_62_FLAVORTEXT: Crios once swore that if anyone could beat him in a duel, he'd give them this staff. Looks like you did it!
WEAPON_63_FLAVORTEXT: The central stone for this staff can only be gathered during storms on the coldest winter nights by the Shiverchill Tribes.
WEAPON_64_FLAVORTEXT: Forged in the heat of the Bonfire Spire, it inspires both fear and awe in anyone who opposes it.
WEAPON_65_FLAVORTEXT: This was a favorite wand among pirate children!
WEAPON_66_FLAVORTEXT: The stoic turtle figure on this wand was made to honor a wise old tortoise.
WEAPON_67_FLAVORTEXT: This little remote may look normal, but something is awful strange about it. Maybe because it doesn't need batteries...
WEAPON_68_FLAVORTEXT: A gift from the great wizard Mira. Just holding this staff makes you feel stronger than ever before.
WEAPON_69_FLAVORTEXT: Not a very powerful weapon, but certainly useful for tightening bolts!
WEAPON_70_FLAVORTEXT: A modified machine, the gizmotron stores magic inside and releases it at the user's will.
WEAPON_71_FLAVORTEXT: A real to life replica of the original Pumpkin Lord's weapon of choice...
WEAPON_72_FLAVORTEXT: There's nothing really wrong with this candle, but it does kind of give you the creeps.
WEAPON_73_FLAVORTEXT: A mystic hammer wielded by the great Frost Beard - some say the hammer grants its wielder the powers of a Norse god.
WEAPON_74_FLAVORTEXT: A standard-issue pickaxe, popular among the miners who used to inhabit the Shiverchill mines.
WEAPON_75_FLAVORTEXT: This staff likely used to be much more powerful...when it worked. Now, it just seems to be used to intimidate.
WEAPON_76_FLAVORTEXT: This strange orb seems to have a small elemental stuck inside, though it seems happy enough in there.
WEAPON_77_FLAVORTEXT: There's something about this staff that you don't like - as if strange whispers beckon you to hold it and never let go.
WEAPON_78_FLAVORTEXT: This weapon is wielded only by the greatest of Bounty Hunters. Nearby monsters are drawn to its crystalline orb with a hypnotic pull.
WEAPON_79_FLAVORTEXT: The standard-issue weapon of many an explorer, it's claw is perfect for getting to those hard to reach places.
WEAPON_80_FLAVORTEXT: One of the only wands that not only increases spell power but also provides an occasional snack.
WEAPON_81_FLAVORTEXT: This is the primary tool used by the Candle Knights. Like the helmet, the light on this candle can never be extinguished.
WEAPON_82_FLAVORTEXT: This was made by Big Hex himself!
WEAPON_83_FLAVORTEXT: This rod was forged by the Warden that gave Arctursus his frost gauntlets.
WEAPON_84_FLAVORTEXT: This wand was grown in the same garden as Florafox. It is said to hold the same plant-healing magic that Florafox can create.
WEAPON_85_FLAVORTEXT: This staff was crafted by townsfolk that lived on the eastern shore of the island, in the hopes that it would help them protect their waters as Diveodile did.
WEAPON_86_FLAVORTEXT: The rod was fashioned out of a stalagmite made of magma in a deep, hidden cave in Bonfire Spire.
WEAPON_88_FLAVORTEXT: This magical blade was created by fairies for other creatures who defend their forest.
WEAPON_89_FLAVORTEXT: A wacky old wizard thought that vegetables had some hidden magical power, and so they created this staff. Maybe they were right?
WEAPON_90_FLAVORTEXT: Whack enemies and protect yourself from rain!
WEAPON_91_FLAVORTEXT: Legend says these swords belonged to the Sky Knights who fought on the backs of Pegasi a long time ago.
WEAPON_92_FLAVORTEXT: Designed to harness magical spells by using the shape of a jellyfish as a focal point.
WEAPON_93_FLAVORTEXT: It was once said that this blade was made for an evil man who had more than 5 fingers!
WEAPON_94_FLAVORTEXT: Not the most powerful wand out there, but people who live under the sea love it for its style.
WEAPON_95_FLAVORTEXT: This ancient wand had been lost in the ocean many years ago after a battle with the Order of Influence.
WEAPON_97_FLAVORTEXT: A magical wand created by the Fairies of Firefly Forest.
WEAPON_98_FLAVORTEXT: These swords appeared in Firefly Forest, allegedly from another dimension!
WEAPON_99_FLAVORTEXT: Forged in the sheer cold of a Shiverchill night on the mountainside. These were the magic-casters of choice for many who lived around the mountain.
WEAPON_100_FLAVORTEXT: It casts magic, and can flip burgers. What more could you want?
WEAPON_101_FLAVORTEXT: At the very tip of the wand is where the magic is strongest.
WEAPON_102_FLAVORTEXT: A double-edged spell-sword that blends Chill & Char's opposing elements.
WEAPON_103_FLAVORTEXT: Feel the fury of the depths with each swing of this heroic weapon.
WEAPON_104_FLAVORTEXT: A small dragon curls around this baton, lending his power to every spell you cast.
WEAPON_105_FLAVORTEXT: Flames shoot from your hand as you cast spell after spell with this sick Stick of Dragonfire!
WEAPON_106_FLAVORTEXT: Wield the full fury of dragonfire with this lance of elemental power.
WEAPON_107_FLAVORTEXT: Harness the sun fire of Luma with this shining bladed wand of power.
WEAPON_108_FLAVORTEXT: The curve of the moon against the darkness of space are captured in this impressive battle weapon.
WEAPON_109_FLAVORTEXT: Do you feel pucky? Well...DO YA??? That might be a sticky question, but here is the sticky answer.
WEAPON_111_FLAVORTEXT: Become one with the snow as you glide fast as the north wind. Part of the elite Ice Crystal set.
WEAPON_112_FLAVORTEXT: How could it be winter without one of these in your hand? Great for clearing paths or building snow forts.
WEAPON_113_FLAVORTEXT: The ultimate warrior weapon for those wishing to strike down Elemental Titans.
WEAPON_114_FLAVORTEXT: The giant emerald used in this hammer is said to have come from a mysterious underground cave deep within Firefly Forest.
WEAPON_115_FLAVORTEXT: A sturdy and reliable mace that accompanied many Skywatch wizards into battle.
WEAPON_116_FLAVORTEXT: This powerful axe was once held by a mighty winged warrior. It was specially crafted to feel as light as a feather.
WEAPON_118_FLAVORTEXT: Forged by the most skilled smiths of Skywatch to defend against the strongest of flying foes.
WEAPON_119_FLAVORTEXT: You can see the sun's bright reflection on this spear as it is raised into the air in celebration.
WEAPON_120_FLAVORTEXT: A double forked spear designed to pin down Elemental Titans during combat.
WEAPON_121_FLAVORTEXT: Swing this bright blade like a fox tail in the wind, showing all foes that Foxes are the bravest fighters in the land.
WEAPON_122_FLAVORTEXT: Don Zorro himself designed this fabulous Fox Knight Blade. It is almost as handsome as he is!
WEAPON_123_FLAVORTEXT: Is it a weapon or the biggest snack you have ever seen? A Bunny always keeps 'em guessing.
WEAPON_124_FLAVORTEXT: A Bunny Ranger is always ready for anything. This blade is perfect for battling monsters or chopping carrots. Made of 100% recycled materials.
WEAPON_125_FLAVORTEXT: What a yummy looking wand. Too bad that you can't eat it! Maybe just a small bite...ouch, ice cream headache!
WEAPON_126_FLAVORTEXT: Comes from a village where the students train to hide with great patience in the leaves.
WEAPON_127_FLAVORTEXT: An epic wand with beastly powers that brings out the animal inside.
WEAPON_128_FLAVORTEXT: Those who wish to wield this weapon must first be strong of heart. Very few pass the test.
WEAPON_129_FLAVORTEXT: A wand with many strengths and few weaknesses.
WEAPON_130_FLAVORTEXT: With an impressive reach, this wand can keep foes at a great distance.
WEAPON_131_FLAVORTEXT: Sing your spells and make musical magic...
WEAPON_132_FLAVORTEXT: A very smart mage designed this mace. As one can see, each spike as a point.
WEAPON_133_FLAVORTEXT: A great spear to battle against sea monsters. Can be thrown or waved about madly.
WEAPON_134_FLAVORTEXT: A true secret weapon, no one expects to face this wand disguised as an umbrella in a battle. Surprise!
WEAPON_135_FLAVORTEXT: If you swing this wand around in the dark it looks really cool. Look at it glow!
WEAPON_136_FLAVORTEXT: Show off your skills as a true Archivist with this Archivist wand!
WEAPON_138_FLAVORTEXT: Be ready for Academy studies with this Academy-issued wand.
WEAPON_139_FLAVORTEXT: The chocolate grows back after every bite. The first bite doesn't count.
WEAPON_140_FLAVORTEXT: The great Celestial Wand gathers powers by moonlight to save the world by daylight!
WEAPON_141_FLAVORTEXT: Harness the energy of the most powerful fire-breathers in the galaxy with this powerful wand.
WEAPON_142_FLAVORTEXT: A special type of crystal can shine a bright light when in the hand of a wizard.
WEAPON_143_FLAVORTEXT: Hold the mighty power of the triceratops in your hand!
WEAPON_144_FLAVORTEXT: Mother earth tells you to be careful with this weapon, or you just might poke your eye out!
WEAPON_148_FLAVORTEXT: Splatter your opponents with slime, paint, or goo! Only if they're ok with it.
WEAPON_150_FLAVORTEXT: Chocoholics unite! Now you can cast your spells and enjoy a sweet taste at the same time.
WEAPON_151_FLAVORTEXT: A legendary flute whose tunes once enchanted the island - studded with the rarest stones!
WEAPON_152_FLAVORTEXT: A special scepter granted to Members for their bravery and commitment.
WEAPON_153_FLAVORTEXT: A massive hammer of explosive power used for devastating strikes against Elemental Titans.
WEAPON_154_FLAVORTEXT: Yetis named the Aries constellation after the rams of Shiverchill Mountains, due to their respect for the rams' tolerance of the cold.
WEAPON_155_FLAVORTEXT: Floatlings admire bulls for their large bodies, much different from theirs. They named the constellation Taurus because of its bull-like shape.
WEAPON_156_FLAVORTEXT: The Gemini constellation was named after a pair of twin wizards, each of whom wielded two wands at once.
WEAPON_157_FLAVORTEXT: Pirates named the constellation Cancer after crabs that scuttle around on the beaches of Shipwreck Shore.
WEAPON_158_FLAVORTEXT: The Floatlings named the constellation Leo after the lion, admiring its courage and bravery.
WEAPON_159_FLAVORTEXT: It is said the constellation Virgo was named after an Earth fairy who filled Lamplight Town with flowers in spring.
WEAPON_160_FLAVORTEXT: The first judge ever was a wizard named Libra. She was so well liked that wizards named the constellation after her in admiration.
WEAPON_161_FLAVORTEXT: Some wizards say that when the constellation Scorpio is in the sky, you can hear Scorpio's music faintly on the wind.
WEAPON_162_FLAVORTEXT: Sagittarius was known for casting spells using his bow and arrows. Wizards tell stories of him when gazing at the constellation.
WEAPON_163_FLAVORTEXT: The constellation Capricorn was named after a legendary sea goat who once saved a drowning wizard.
WEAPON_164_FLAVORTEXT: The constellation Aquarius was named after a powerful wizard known to heal others with a jug of magical water.
WEAPON_165_FLAVORTEXT: The Pisces constellation was named after a pair of large fish in Shipwreck Shore, who would often guide sailors lost at sea.
WEAPON_169_FLAVORTEXT: This magnificent beast of a wand allows the caster to summon the full roaring strength of the sea.
WEAPON_170_FLAVORTEXT: This wand doesn't only cast spells; it plays rock music, too!
WEAPON_171_FLAVORTEXT: This magical key has been said to unlock almost anything, but it can't unlock hearts.
WEAPON_172_FLAVORTEXT: Wacky and colorful, engage your opponents with this summer-themed pool-noodle sword.
WEAPON_173_FLAVORTEXT: This magical pencil's tip will never break.
WEAPON_174_FLAVORTEXT: The hook shape of this candy-cane wand makes it easy to hang up when you're not using it.
WEAPON_175_FLAVORTEXT: Passed down in the Von Loot family, this lightweight and trendy scepter is the ultimate symbol of a rogue prince.
WEAPON_176_FLAVORTEXT: This sickle was crafted by miners years ago, using the finest and toughest crystals they could find. It's said to be extremely powerful.
WEAPON_177_FLAVORTEXT: Summon a strong Ice spell to freeze your foes with this oversized wand.
WEAPON_178_FLAVORTEXT: What an old and mysterious wand. You can feel The Ancient's power running through it.
WEAPON_179_FLAVORTEXT: Summon a squall of snowflakes to strike down!
WEAPON_180_FLAVORTEXT: Shake up a snowstorm to freeze your opponents.
WEAPON_181_FLAVORTEXT: The lil' snowman atop this wand has been said to do a lil' dance when wizards win battles.
WEAPON_182_FLAVORTEXT: The staff's gem vibrates with terrible energy during Elemental Titan battles with the wings amplifying the users innate powers.
WEAPON_183_FLAVORTEXT: A shining Lance of absolute precision it can shatter the defense of any Elemental Titan it's used against.
WEAPON_184_FLAVORTEXT: Powerful, but slightly unstable, energy can be seen leaking out of the purified Plant Titan Shard at the tip.
WEAPON_185_FLAVORTEXT: Purified Fire Titan Shards grant extra power and strength to this massive magma stone blade.
WEAPON_187_FLAVORTEXT: A trident of purified Water Titan Shards, when used its awesome power is akin to crashing waves.
WEAPON_188_FLAVORTEXT: The crook keeps the infused Storm Titan Shards' power at bay from the user.
WEAPON_189_FLAVORTEXT: A purified Ice Titan Shard rests in the center of this icy staff.
WEAPON_190_FLAVORTEXT: When this wand catches the light, it turns it into a beautiful rainbow
WEAPON_191_FLAVORTEXT: What does a wizard like more than casting spells? Blowing bubbles AND casting spells!
WEAPON_192_FLAVORTEXT: This magical bat will hit your spells right out of the park and give them wings!
WEAPON_193_FLAVORTEXT: You know the party is about to start when you have the Concert Light-stick.
WEAPON_194_FLAVORTEXT: This used to be an ordinary pointy rock until The Ancient's great power was bestowed upon it.
WEAPON_195_FLAVORTEXT: During a windy day the Pin Wheel wand spins. Legend has it the faster it spins, the stronger the wizard.
WEAPON_196_FLAVORTEXT: The Dual Blade is twice as sharp and twice as fast. Be careful when handling this powerful sword.
WEAPON_197_FLAVORTEXT: This Tech Hammer is the most advance hammer wand there is. Can you handle it's advance power.
WEAPON_198_FLAVORTEXT: This wand casts spells like a dream.
WEAPON_199_FLAVORTEXT: Don't worry, this wand won't bite.  We think.
WEAPON_200_FLAVORTEXT: Discover the secret to the universe with your Atom Wand
WEAPON_201_FLAVORTEXT: Refreshing during the warm summer days!
WEAPON_202_FLAVORTEXT: Cuts through solid ice like... solid ice. Fortunately, it's also a powerful wand.
WEAPON_203_FLAVORTEXT: What better way to catch flies than with one of these!
WEAPON_204_FLAVORTEXT: A sturdy wooden wand. They don't make them like this anymore.
WEAPON_205_FLAVORTEXT: A plastic sword used for holding sandwiches together, picking your teeth, and defeating your enemies!
WEAPON_206_FLAVORTEXT: A piece of fried chicken seasoned to perfection! Careful not to burn yourself, it's still hot.
WEAPON_208_FLAVORTEXT: This steam powered sword rattles with energy. Can you handle the pressure?
WEAPON_209_FLAVORTEXT: What does a wizard like more than casting spells? Blowing bubbles AND casting spells! Get the Bubble Wand now in Pink!
WEAPON_210_FLAVORTEXT: This looks like a wing from a powerful dragon. Can you handle the heat?
WEAPON_211_FLAVORTEXT: This magical parasol gathers the power of the sun, just don't forget the colors.
WEAPON_212_FLAVORTEXT: This is no ordinary Fungus. This rare wand was made in the deepest parts of Firefly Forest. Enjoy casting powerful spells with this Magic Fungus.
WEAPON_213_FLAVORTEXT: Very useful to show you care during picnics by cutting sandwiches with hard crusts in exchange for group hugs.
WEAPON_214_FLAVORTEXT: This axe reminds you to be proud. Inside you there is royalty and you must remember this.
WEAPON_215_FLAVORTEXT: Legend has it that this gem used to belong to a powerful ice dragon. Can you handle its ice powers?
WEAPON_216_FLAVORTEXT: The Cactus Club is very powerful but also very dangerous. Be careful not to poke your eye out when wielding its mighty power.
WEAPON_217_FLAVORTEXT: Some firebirds bring life, others bring destruction.
WEAPON_218_FLAVORTEXT: Wands were not always elegant, but even in the past they packed a wallop!
WEAPON_219_FLAVORTEXT: Control the power of a Luminex in the palm of your hands with the Luminex Claw.
WEAPON_221_FLAVORTEXT: Some birds living in the forest learn to control the wind between the trees.
WEAPON_222_FLAVORTEXT: The Icebird sings a song that is so beautiful it gives you the chills.
WEAPON_223_FLAVORTEXT: Stars are a source of magic, and while most wizards look to the skies, some look to the ocean floor.
WEAPON_224_FLAVORTEXT: Become the master of wind and clouds. The Sky Staff will take you to new heights.
WEAPON_226_FLAVORTEXT: A tool made for hurling snowballs at a high speed.
WEAPON_227_FLAVORTEXT: The head of this hammer is made of actual moon rock. You can feel a pull of gravity around it.
WEAPON_228_FLAVORTEXT: A tool used for mining asteroids in outer space under some extremely frightening circumstances.
WEAPON_229_FLAVORTEXT: A sword made using alien science. The blade is not physical, it is a gateway into outer space.
WEAPON_230_FLAVORTEXT: It is true that when you walk with a lantern in the dark you will see your way, but you will no longer see the stars.
WEAPON_231_FLAVORTEXT: It's important to be like a kite and rise higher when the wind is against you. Usually wizards with this wand are relaxed but ready for anything.
WEAPON_233_FLAVORTEXT: A harp can be as sharp as a sword if it plays a song that is true.
WEAPON_234_FLAVORTEXT: Have a big smile and laugh often. This wand helps you remember the good times.
WEAPON_235_FLAVORTEXT: So what if you dropped it a couple of times? You had things on your mind and it still works, so it's fine!
WEAPON_236_FLAVORTEXT: Sometimes music can be heard in the halls of the Dark Tower. The low notes played from this harp sound just like it.
WEAPON_237_FLAVORTEXT: Victory has never tasted so good. Win or lose battles with S'mores wand is a treat.
WEAPON_238_FLAVORTEXT: It's pretty much as dangerous to the user as it is to the enemy. Be careful how you hold it and absolutely never run with it.
WEAPON_239_FLAVORTEXT: Named for the sound it makes when it hits. The boom can be heard from far away.
WEAPON_240_FLAVORTEXT: A blade that requires skill to use, and because of its size, skill to avoid. Carry it carefully!
WEAPON_241_FLAVORTEXT: This sword was made for two reasons: to win battles and to send a message while you do it.
WEAPON_242_FLAVORTEXT: Heavy-duty and delicious. The one who carries this wand will win many battles but then they will likely accidentally eat the wand itself.
WEAPON_243_FLAVORTEXT: Legend says this sword dropped out of the sky during a lightning storm. Can one wizard handle all this power?
WEAPON_244_FLAVORTEXT: Catch your enemies off guard and send them reeling with this long wand made to look like a fishing rod. They'll fall for it hook, line, and sinker.
WEAPON_245_FLAVORTEXT: The long and pointy nose of a swordfish makes for an excellent blade. It swims through the air to hit its mark.
WEAPON_246_FLAVORTEXT: A tool that pushes and pulls the water to flush your problems away.
WEAPON_247_FLAVORTEXT: This warrior beetle has decided to lend you its strength. Now you can bring the power of a true warrior beetle into battle with you.
WEAPON_248_FLAVORTEXT: A special hook pistol used by a legendary pirate with both arms intact known as Armful Arnie.
WEAPON_250_FLAVORTEXT: An ancient magic was trapped in this amber 200 million years ago during the age of the dinosaurs.
WEAPON_251_FLAVORTEXT: The wand of the Puppet Master. Now that the negative energy has been removed, leaves have begun to grow from its roots.
WEAPON_252_FLAVORTEXT: This wand still hasn't found its true master.
WEAPON_253_FLAVORTEXT: A barb taken from the ancient Force Drake was used as the head of this spear!
WEAPON_254_FLAVORTEXT: The claws of the Might Wyrm are rumored to never dull! Who knows if that's true, but this blade certainly is sharp!
WEAPON_255_FLAVORTEXT: Blinding flashes of light are released whenever this mace strikes a surface even gently!
WEAPON_256_FLAVORTEXT: A beautiful crystiline planet glows softly with astral power while magically suspended at the top of this staff.
WEAPON_257_FLAVORTEXT: The edge of a tooth of the ancient Night Wyrm is razor sharp, its shadow power allowing it to cut through any material.
WEAPON_258_FLAVORTEXT: Shadows billow off this wand as though it was cold steam.
WEAPON_259_FLAVORTEXT: Silver wand made for those with gumption.
WEAPON_260_FLAVORTEXT: Gold wand made for those with gumption.
WEAPON_261_FLAVORTEXT: Platinum wand made for those with gumption.
WEAPON_262_FLAVORTEXT: Thrice tempered in golden carrot juice, it's as sharp as its glint.
WEAPON_263_FLAVORTEXT: Made from a special carrot-beet hybrid, extra sweet for when you're thrust into a prickly situation.
WEAPON_264_FLAVORTEXT: Using a special blue alloy known only to a secret clad of fox smith masters.
WEAPON_265_FLAVORTEXT: Also known as silver fox blades, they're highly coveted but owned by very few.
WEAPON_266_FLAVORTEXT: A magical wand said to contain the luck of the little people.
WEAPON_267_FLAVORTEXT: A magical blade forged by wizards of old using the power of the sun.
WEAPON_268_FLAVORTEXT: A wand made from wood and mushrooms that have stood for hundreds of years.
WEAPON_269_FLAVORTEXT: Its cut is like the fiercest of northerly winds and absorbs ice to power its spell.
WEAPON_270_FLAVORTEXT: A petrified Spicefire pepper forms its core, still sizzling with spiciness.
WEAPON_271_FLAVORTEXT: Pulled from a long gone civilization lost to the ocean depths.
WEAPON_272_FLAVORTEXT: Clouds and lightning bolts were hammered together via archaic spells to craft this.
WEAPON_273_FLAVORTEXT: A hammer of dark powers that squeeks when you smash something with it.
WEAPON_274_FLAVORTEXT: Special wand used by some rival wizards.
WEAPON_275_FLAVORTEXT: Special wand used by some rival fire wizards.
WEAPON_276_FLAVORTEXT: Special wand used by some rival ice wizards.
WEAPON_277_FLAVORTEXT: Special wand used by some rival plant wizards.
WEAPON_278_FLAVORTEXT: Special wand used by some rival storm wizards.
WEAPON_279_FLAVORTEXT: Special wand used by some rival water wizards.
WEAPON_280_FLAVORTEXT: Special wand used by some rival shadow wizards.
WEAPON_281_FLAVORTEXT: Special wand used by some rival astral wizards.
WEAPON_282_FLAVORTEXT: Made from concentrated magical fruit juice, it's a wand and it quenches your thirst at the same time.
WEAPON_283_FLAVORTEXT: A wand formed from ice gathered at Serrazug & Boreas dancing grounds, filled with icy magic.
WEAPON_284_FLAVORTEXT: One, two skewer! Keep the fruity victims as a snack for later.
WEAPON_285_FLAVORTEXT: The bamboo bends in the howling winds but never breaks.
WEAPON_286_FLAVORTEXT: Friend to the wizard who travels far from home, it comforts a lonely heart and smells of fresh bread.
WEAPON_287_FLAVORTEXT: Comes with a custom scoop handle with a one size fits all policy.
WEAPON_288_FLAVORTEXT: A burning Flame Sapphire taken from a Flame Drake's treasure trove sits at the tip of this powerful fire staff.
WEAPON_289_FLAVORTEXT: The magma tooth of an ancient Inferno Wyrm is the base for this searing blade.
WEAPON_290_FLAVORTEXT: This shadow infused staff draws on the power of rams and their inscrutable and strange nature.
WEAPON_291_FLAVORTEXT: The Taurus Dark Wand channels the bull's anger and explosive strength.
WEAPON_292_FLAVORTEXT: The shadow magic in this wand draws its power from the depths of the ocean where certain crabs live.
WEAPON_293_FLAVORTEXT: The dark magics of this wand are greatly amplified when used in pairs especially when wielded by twins.
WEAPON_294_FLAVORTEXT: The Leo Dark Wand draws its power from the Lion's power during nightime ambushes.
WEAPON_295_FLAVORTEXT: This wand draws its power from strangling vines and other plants overwhelming the forest.
WEAPON_296_FLAVORTEXT: The Libra Dark Wand draws it's shadowy powers from inequality and injustice.
WEAPON_297_FLAVORTEXT: The deadly poisonous sting of Scorpio powers this wand of darkness.
WEAPON_298_FLAVORTEXT: The Saggitarius Dark Wand uses the feelings of loneliness and nothingness to power its spells.
WEAPON_299_FLAVORTEXT: This dark wand draws power from all those lost at sea beneath the towering black waves.
WEAPON_300_FLAVORTEXT: The shadowy magics in this wand originate from the chaotic and unpredictable nature of water in its liquid form.
WEAPON_301_FLAVORTEXT: The Pisces Dark Wand draws power from all the scary and terrible fish creatures lurking in the dark depths.
WEAPON_302_FLAVORTEXT: A fancy pitchfork that channels lightning through the holder....ZAP!
WEAPON_303_FLAVORTEXT: Special ceremonial weapon wielded by ancient pharaohs.
WEAPON_304_FLAVORTEXT: Used to reap the harvest of grains at their ripest.
WEAPON_305_FLAVORTEXT: So sharp it can cut through even the shadows themselves.
WEAPON_306_FLAVORTEXT: A magical lance given to the guards who watch over the Autumn King's court.
WEAPON_307_FLAVORTEXT: Mystical broom filled with Pumpkinfest magic which belonged to the wizard who started festivals.
WEAPON_308_FLAVORTEXT: A very soft and fluffy wand in the shape of a furry Luminite paw.
WEAPON_309_FLAVORTEXT: Magic shovel used by Snowmen to patch themselves if they happen to melt a little.
WEAPON_310_FLAVORTEXT: A stylish cane to go with an elegant ensemble that makes a person want to say, "Bah! Humbug"!
WEAPON_314_FLAVORTEXT: A wand so dark even the Puppet Master was nervous when using it.
WEAPON_315_FLAVORTEXT: A wand used only on rare occasions usually during cherry blossom or other flowering festivals.
WEAPON_316_FLAVORTEXT: A bamboo sword as powerful as a tiger's roar.
WEAPON_317_FLAVORTEXT: Lunar dancers spin and twirl this baton during their mesmerizing performances.
WEAPON_318_FLAVORTEXT: Sleight of hand and illusions are this wands speciality.
WEAPON_319_FLAVORTEXT: This little monkey is ready for some hectic fun.
WEAPON_320_FLAVORTEXT: A sliver of unyielding ice from the nail of an ancient Glacier Wyrm.
WEAPON_321_FLAVORTEXT: The breath of a Frost Drake resides in the splendid crystal that adorns this staff.
WEAPON_322_FLAVORTEXT: Aptly named after Poppet's impressive build.
RIFTRUNKEY_1_FLAVORTEXT: A strange item that looks like a key. Opens a rift on Dragon Isle.
RIFTRUNKEY_2_FLAVORTEXT: A strange item that looks like a key. Opens a rift on Dragon Isle.
RIFTRUNKEY_3_FLAVORTEXT: A strange item that looks like a key. Opens a rift on Dragon Isle.

FOLLOW_329_FLAVORTEXT: Cryospikes roam around on frosty nights looking for their favorite food - frozen berries. When the season warms up, they hibernate in their cave dens.
FOLLOW_330_FLAVORTEXT: Verdama's fur is squishy soft. Naturally friendly, they use the moss on their back to moisturize and exfoliate.
ITEM_278_FLAVORTEXT: Morphs you into an enchanted book for 10 mins.
ITEM_279_FLAVORTEXT: Morphs you into a Glow-worm for 10 minutes.
MOUNT_43_FLAVORTEXT: Discover a whole new world of 1001 Arabian Nights.
PET_261_FLAVORTEXT:
Type: Protector

Protectors have high defense, health & heal allies.

They often build their homes on well ventilated asteroids.
PET_262_FLAVORTEXT:
Type: Protector

Protectors have high defense, health & heal allies.

They are great explorers of the stars and often hitch rides on passing comets to move between systems.
PET_339_FLAVORTEXT:
Type: Wild

Wild pets are powerful and fast but are hard to control.

When a Bonabite's teeth grow in, it must leave the boneyard for suitable prey.
PET_341_FLAVORTEXT:
Type: Protector

Protectors have high defense, health & heal allies.

While they are superb  swimmers, Aquanauts only walk on land after they gain confidence.
PET_342_FLAVORTEXT:
Type: Protector

Protectors have high defense, health & heal allies.

Equally adept on land and in water, an Aquanator is the ideal beach pet.
PET_387_FLAVORTEXT:
Type: Protector

Protectors have high defense, health & heal allies.

Verdama's fur is squishy soft. Naturally friendly, they use the moss on their back to moisturize and exfoliate.
PETHAT_128_FLAVORTEXT: Your pet won't just look like the ocean; it'll smell like it, too!
PETHAT_129_FLAVORTEXT: We see seashells that she sells on Shipwreck Shore.
PETHAT_130_FLAVORTEXT: Maybe there's still sand in it, but it does protect from the sun.
PETHAT_131_FLAVORTEXT: It's a jar of ink. Pink ink made of zinc. Hoodwink me to drink pink zinc ink? I'm on the brink, I think.
PETHAT_139_FLAVORTEXT: Cryospike crystals must be cold-welded to its back.
PETRELIC_133_FLAVORTEXT: Spiral shells from Shipwreck Shore shimmer in the spring sun.
PETRELIC_134_FLAVORTEXT: Beauty may be subjective, but the smell is universal.
PETRELIC_141_FLAVORTEXT: Forged in the coldest frost, Cryospike crystals are smithed by hand.

BOOTS_169_FLAVORTEXT: So fancy that even fancy wonders if it's fancy enough.

FOLLOW_199_FLAVORTEXT: Surfing Kitty is one cool cat.
FOLLOW_218_FLAVORTEXT: Bats like to live in large groups. It seems like this group has taken a liking to you.
FOLLOW_232_FLAVORTEXT: Snowballs have been known to stick to certain wizards.
FOLLOW_234_FLAVORTEXT: Having this alien following you around is out of this world.
FOLLOW_331_FLAVORTEXT: Living in thunderheads next to Stormbluffs, Thunderclaws guide lightning strikes as a mating display.
CURRENCIES_1_FLAVORTEXT: The gold standard for shopping. Earn these from quests, battles, and more!
CURRENCIES_2_FLAVORTEXT: The primary currency of Pumpkinfest. Used to buy costumes and cool items in the town square during the event!
CURRENCIES_3_FLAVORTEXT: A rusty old coin. Used to buy rare artifacts from the wandering merchant.
CURRENCIES_4_FLAVORTEXT: The primary currency of the Starlight Festival. These are used to buy costumes and cool items in the town square during the event!
CURRENCIES_5_FLAVORTEXT: The primary currency of Winterfest. These are used to buy costumes and cool items in the town square during the event!
CURRENCIES_9_FLAVORTEXT: This special wood was used by Floatlings to construct makeshift houses as they traveled around the island in search of knowledge.
CURRENCIES_10_FLAVORTEXT: These captured Dragon Flies can be traded to Mama Star for special dragon-ish items.
CURRENCIES_11_FLAVORTEXT: Shards broken off of a Titan created by the Order of Influence. Can be given to a Floatling to have the Shadow magic removed from it, in exchange for rare items.
CURRENCIES_12_FLAVORTEXT: Adventure in Bonfire Spire to earn these lava chips, then spend them at the Hot Hot Threads shop.
CURRENCIES_13_FLAVORTEXT: Adventure in Firefly Forest to earn these woodland coins, then spend them at the Firefly Outfitters shop.
CURRENCIES_14_FLAVORTEXT: Adventure in Shipwreck Shores to earn these pirate coins, then spend them at the Ocean Outfitters shop.
CURRENCIES_15_FLAVORTEXT: Adventure in Shiverchill Mountains to earn these icy coins, then spend them at the Snowday Sales shop.
CURRENCIES_16_FLAVORTEXT: Adventure in Skywatch to earn these mechanical coins, then spend them at the Sky-High Values shop.
CURRENCIES_17_FLAVORTEXT: These magical eggs are only found when the Springfest is active. They can be collected by adventuring or performing Springfest activities. Spend them at the Fox or Bunny shops.
CURRENCIES_18_FLAVORTEXT: The primary currency of the Academy Archives. Five of these are collected per Archive run to summon a prize!
CURRENCIES_19_FLAVORTEXT: Adventure through the Academy's Archives to collect these pages, then exchange them at the Academy's shop.
CURRENCIES_20_FLAVORTEXT: The primary currency of Summerfest. These are used to buy cool items, hair styles, and hair colors in the town square during the event!
CURRENCIES_21_FLAVORTEXT: Samples are bits of elemental magic collected from places. They're studied, then used to build things using the same elements!
CURRENCIES_22_FLAVORTEXT: Samples are bits of elemental magic collected from places. They're studied, then used to build things using the same elements!
CURRENCIES_23_FLAVORTEXT: Samples are bits of elemental magic collected from places. They're studied, then used to build things using the same elements!
CURRENCIES_24_FLAVORTEXT: Samples are bits of elemental magic collected from places. They're studied, then used to build things using the same elements!
CURRENCIES_25_FLAVORTEXT: Samples are bits of elemental magic collected from places. They're studied, then used to build things using the same elements!
CURRENCIES_27_FLAVORTEXT: Magicoin is a special currency found in Treasure Track. Use them to evovle your pets and capture new ones.
CURRENCIES_28_FLAVORTEXT: A dragon scale from a mighty dragon. Collect these by completing Rifts and use them to buy cool items in the Rift Shop!
CURRENCIES_29_FLAVORTEXT: A dragon scale that shines bright but seems it won’t shine for very long. Get it by completing the time-limited Rifts and trade it for cool items in the Rift Shop
CURRENCIES_30_FLAVORTEXT: A dragon scale that shines bright but seems it won’t shine for very long. Get it by completing the time-limited Rifts and trade it for cool items in the Rift Shop
CURRENCIES_31_FLAVORTEXT: The primary currency of Moonlight Fest. These are used to buy cool items, hair styles, and hair colors in the town square during the event!
DORM_228_FLAVORTEXT: Bats like to live in large groups. Sometimes they take up resence in your home.
ITEM_196_FLAVORTEXT: With a burst of multiple colors the smell of falling autumn leaves fills the air.
MOUNT_4_FLAVORTEXT: What's better than a pumpkin pie? How about a pumpkin that can fly! Soar through the skies in your Jack-o'-mount.

Type: Striker

Strikers excel at Crits, Dodge & Power.

Known for their terrifying howling in the dark, Nighthowls are extremly loyal to their pack.
PET_279_FLAVORTEXT:
Type: Striker

Strikers excel at Crits, Dodge & Power.

Veldents are able to burrow through sheer rock using their teeth and front paws.
PET_281_FLAVORTEXT:
Type: Speedy

Speedy pets are fast and excel at Multi Hit attacks.

Coming in contact will give you 1st degree burns, and that’s in the water.
PET_282_FLAVORTEXT:
Type: Versatile

Versatile pets are good at everything a little bit.

The favourite haunts of Spiricolts include quiet secluded spots where they can graze in peace.
PET_283_FLAVORTEXT:
Type: Versatile

Versatile pets are good at everything a little bit.

Mystigeist love to gallop at incredible speeds, racing each other in the darkness of the night.
PET_284_FLAVORTEXT:
Type: Versatile

Versatile pets are good at everything a little bit.

Eidolorns are so mysterious that most people don't believe they exist. The few who have seen them tell stories of their majesty and might.
PET_314_FLAVORTEXT:
Type: Striker

Strikers excel at Crits, Dodge & Power.

They have the softest paws that are perfect for running silently without being seen or heard.
PET_315_FLAVORTEXT:
Type: Striker

Strikers excel at Crits, Dodge & Power.

Very shy, they will turn tail and run if noticed, but not before alerting others of the potential danger.

Type: Protector

Protectors have high defense, health & heal allies.

Mechanites build living gear constructs in the most bizarre places including outer space, in the air and even in underground caverns.
